q: Mutualism — zooxanthellae and coral: Which correctly describes this named mutualistic relationship?
options: Zooxanthellae are parasitic algae that steal nutrients from coral polyps, weakening their calcification

Zooxanthellae are photosynthetic dinoflagellates living in coral tissue; they provide the coral with photosynthetically produced carbohydrates, while the coral provides protection and CO2 for photosynthesis
Zooxanthellae are nitrogen-fixing bacteria that convert atmospheric N2 into ammonium for coral growth
Zooxanthellae are fungi that decompose dead coral tissue, releasing nutrients back into the reef ecosystem
explanation: Zooxanthellae are symbiotic algae inside coral cells; they photosynthesize and supply up to 90% of the coral's energy needs as organic carbon, while coral provides shelter and metabolic CO2.
